mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes yield positive 20-day SPX returns during expansions, or the predictive coefficient flips sign without regime change.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread fails to predict 5-day returns when above 2.0, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate income and consumption capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative payroll revisions consistently coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns, or the signal shows zero autocorrelation with forward returns over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic momentum that lifts corporate profit forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 0.6% QoQ fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production during high-inflation regimes signals supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during high inflation yield positive 5-day SPX returns, or the mechanism loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL MoM falls below 3%.
